sourceMovie.title: Labor Day
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Labor Day works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Labor Day clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Labor Day?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Room, Nocturnal Animals—each shares DNA with Labor Day while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Labor Day?
faq.answer: Labor Day (2013) is best described as Drama, Romance, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Labor Day worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
